analysis_window.notes: Default analysis scope begins when structured race preparation started.

data_collection_protocol: Record every ride on the Garmin when possible.
Keep wearing the Apple Watch for sleep, HRV, resting HR, and general recovery metrics.
Use Apple Health as a recovery and secondary workout source, not as the primary bike-analysis source when Garmin FIT files exist.

limitations: No current injuries that preclude workouts.

primary_power_exercise.name: explosive walking lunges
primary_power_exercise.description: Walking lunges with dumbbells in a cycling-style bent posture.

lab_profiles.source: Dr. Max Testa / Intermountain Sports Medicine Park City Hospital
notes: Use this lab profile as the current physiological baseline.
Treat threshold differently on flats versus climbing/altitude.
The training zone table is coaching guidance; the summary box contains anchor values from the test sheet.

race_goals.name: Silver Rush 50 MTB
race_goals.terrain: Mainly fire roads with limited singletrack.
goal_notes: High-altitude climbing durability is the main limiter.
Need sustainable power and pacing for a 6-7 hour effort.

race_goals.name: Park City Point 2 Point
race_goals.terrain: About 99% singletrack on Park City trails.

prior_result.notes: Late-August Garmin ride should reflect this event effort.

goal_notes: Need much better long-duration durability, climbing repeatability, and technical-trail pacing than last year.

training_constraints.weekly_schedule_flexibility: Very flexible schedule; can usually find 4-5 hours per day for long rides if needed.
training_constraints.default_assumption: Training can be placed on any day of the week.

long_ride_fueling: Electrolytes and high sodium in drinks
race_day_notes: Liquid calories and gels are more realistic than solid food during hard races.
During the 2025 Point 2 Point, solid food was difficult to stomach mid-race.

memphis.name: zone_2_endurance
structure: 20-30 min easy at less than 130 W
3 x 30 min at 130-170 W with heart rate below 135
5 min easy stop / fuel / drink between 30 min blocks

memphis.name: aerobic_threshold_flats
structure: 4-5 min very gentle spinning between intervals

memphis.name: intermittent_threshold
structure: 2 x 20 min sets as 4 x (3 min hard / 2 min easy)
30 min easy riding between the two 20 min sets
notes: Long warm-up is intentional and should be preserved.
The 30 min easy period between sets is part of the workout.

park_city.name: climbing_intervals_altitude
notes: Dr. Testa described this as VO2 max work.
Treat it as race-specific uphill aerobic-power work at altitude.

current_positive_signals: Recent 40-50 mile Memphis rides have felt strong.
Recent 30 mile rides have felt especially strong and not overly fatiguing.
current_focus_cues: On uphill efforts, athlete is intentionally trying to raise cadence from about 60-70 rpm toward 85-95 rpm to shift the limiter toward the cardiovascular system instead of local leg fatigue.
self_assessment.technical_skill: Confident on descents and technical sections due to substantial trail-riding background.
primary_limiters_from_2025_point_to_poin: total endurance for repeated climbing over many hours
non_limiters_from_2025_point_to_point: Descending confidence was not the problem

analysis_rules: Treat the 2026-03-18 Dr. Testa lactate test as the authoritative baseline until superseded by a newer lab test.
When classifying threshold work, distinguish between flat threshold and climbing/altitude threshold.
Use Garmin FIT data for record-level bike analysis whenever available.
Use Apple Health for readiness, sleep, HRV, resting heart rate, and as a secondary ride source when Garmin data is missing.
Default analysis scope begins on 2025-07-01 unless the user explicitly asks for earlier history.
open_inputs_needed: Exact Memphis bike sensor setup once cadence and/or power sensors are added
